name: Priya S.
title: Finally, ten minutes that count
text: Between meetings and school runs I get maybe ten minutes of focused time with my daughter. These cards make those minutes actually mean something. She asks for them — I never have to push.

name: Daniel K.
title: Times tables without tears
text: We tried apps. We tried worksheets. The traffic-light sorting in this box is the first thing my son has stuck with. He runs his own practice now and shows me his 'green pile' at dinner.

name: Ananya R.
title: The quality surprised me
text: Thick cards, lovely rounded corners, and they've survived six months of a toddler's backpack. You can tell parents designed these — the prompts on the back tell you exactly what to say.

name: Marcus T.
title: Our restaurant secret weapon
text: The riddle cards live in my jacket pocket. Waiting for food used to mean screens; now it's the whole table arguing over a puzzle. Genuinely fun for adults too.

name: Elena V.
title: Helped us through big feelings
text: The emotions cards gave my daughter words for what she feels. 'I'm frustrated, not angry' — from a four-year-old. Worth every penny for that sentence alone.

name: James W.
title: My go-to birthday gift now
text: I've gifted the World Wonders set three times. Beautifully boxed, feels premium, and parents thank me weeks later — which never happened with toys.
